content.content:
1	//! Phase-B reconcile shim: re-exports the canonical `LuaState` from
2	//! `lua-vm` and provides an extension trait holding every method the
3	//! Phase-A stdlib translation used to call on the Phase-A stub.
4	//!
5	//! TODO_ARCH(phase-b-reconcile): all extension-trait method bodies are
6	//! `todo!("phase-b-reconcile: <name>")`. They must move to real
7	//! implementations on `lua_vm::state::LuaState` itself; that work lives in
8	//! `lua-vm`, not here. The shim exists only so stdlib code keeps compiling
9	//! while the canonical `LuaState` API stabilises.
10	//!
11	//! Where a trait method's name collides with an inherent method on the
12	//! canonical `LuaState`, Rust resolves to the inherent method. Most
13	//! Phase-A call sites compile through the inherent method unchanged; the
14	//! handful that depend on a different return shape (e.g. `state.push(...)?`
15	//! against the canonical `pub fn push(&mut self, val: LuaValue)`) are
16	//! patched at the call site.
17	
18	#![allow(dead_code, unused_variables, clippy::too_many_arguments)]
19	
20	use lua_types::{
21	    arith::ArithOp,
22	    error::LuaError,
23	    gc::GcRef,
24	    string::LuaString,
25	    userdata::LuaUserData,
26	    value::{LuaThread, LuaValue},
27	    LuaType,
28	    LuaStatus,
29	};
30	
31	pub use lua_vm::state::LuaState;
32	
33	/// Bare function callable from Lua. C: `lua_CFunction`.
34	#[allow(non_camel_case_types)]
35	pub type lua_CFunction = fn(&mut LuaState) -> Result<usize, LuaError>;
36	
37	/// Pseudo-index for the `i`-th upvalue of a C function.
38	/// C: `#define lua_upvalueindex(i)`
39	pub fn upvalue_index(i: i32) -> i32 {
40	    -1_001_000 - i
41	}
42	
43	/// Comparison operations (eq, lt, le). C: `LUA_OP*`.
44	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
45	pub enum CompareOp {
46	    Eq,
47	    Lt,
48	    Le,
49	}
50	
51	/// Reader-callback type for `lua_load`. C: `lua_Reader`.
52	pub type LuaReader<'a> = dyn FnMut() -> Option<Vec<u8>> + 'a;
53	
54	/// Writer-callback type for `lua_dump`. C: `lua_Writer`.
55	pub type LuaWriter<'a> = dyn FnMut(&[u8]) -> Result<(), LuaError> + 'a;
56	
57	/// Debug introspection record. C: `lua_Debug`.
58	#[derive(Debug, Default, Clone)]
59	pub struct LuaDebug {
60	    pub name: Option<Vec<u8>>,
61	    pub namewhat: Vec<u8>,
62	    pub what: u8,
63	    pub source: Vec<u8>,
64	    pub short_src: Vec<u8>,
65	    pub linedefined: i32,
66	    pub lastlinedefined: i32,
67	    pub currentline: i32,
68	    pub nups: u8,
69	    pub nparams: u8,
70	    pub isvararg: bool,
71	    pub istailcall: bool,
72	    pub ftransfer: u16,
73	    pub ntransfer: u16,
74	}
75	
76	impl LuaDebug {
77	    pub fn name_bytes(&self) -> &[u8] { self.name.as_deref().unwrap_or(b"?") }
78	    pub fn namewhat_bytes(&self) -> &[u8] { &self.namewhat }
79	    pub fn what_bytes(&self) -> &[u8] { match self.what { b'L' => b"Lua", b'C' => b"C", b'm' => b"main", _ => b"?" } }
80	    pub fn short_src_bytes(&self) -> &[u8] { &self.short_src }
81	    pub fn source_bytes(&self) -> &[u8] { &self.source }
82	}
83	
84	/// Extension trait wiring every Phase-A stub method onto the canonical
85	/// `LuaState`. Bodies are `todo!("phase-b-reconcile: …")`. When the
86	/// canonical type already defines a method with the same name, Rust's
87	/// inherent-first resolution makes this trait method unreachable (the
88	/// inherent one wins) — the trait is then only providing the *missing*
89	/// methods. Conflicting call-sites whose shape no longer matches the
90	/// inherent signature are patched in their respective stdlib modules.

content.text:
Build is green.

**Report:**
- **Modified:** `crates/lua-stdlib/src/state_stub.rs:300-302` — added `abs_index` method to the `impl LuaStateStubExt for LuaState` block.
- **What it does:** Delegates to the canonical `lua_vm::api::abs_index(self, idx)`, which implements C's `lua_absindex`: returns `idx` if already positive or a pseudo-index, else converts a negative stack-relative index to absolute via `top - ci.func + idx`. The canonical impl already exists at `crates/lua-vm/src/api.rs:197`.
- **Inner todo!() calls:** None — `lua_vm::api::abs_index` only touches `state.current_call_info()` and `state.top_idx()`, both already real.
- **cargo build -p lua-cli:** green (warnings only, no errors).

result:
Build is green.

**Report:**
- **Modified:** `crates/lua-stdlib/src/state_stub.rs:300-302` — added `abs_index` method to the `impl LuaStateStubExt for LuaState` block.
- **What it does:** Delegates to the canonical `lua_vm::api::abs_index(self, idx)`, which implements C's `lua_absindex`: returns `idx` if already positive or a pseudo-index, else converts a negative stack-relative index to absolute via `top - ci.func + idx`. The canonical impl already exists at `crates/lua-vm/src/api.rs:197`.
- **Inner todo!() calls:** None — `lua_vm::api::abs_index` only touches `state.current_call_info()` and `state.top_idx()`, both already real.
- **cargo build -p lua-cli:** green (warnings only, no errors).
